chunks.page_summary: This page provides guidance on ordering and implementing Ethernet/IP communication for Rheonics sensors, including details on ordering the protocol, post-shipment ordering, and cabling requirements.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863
Getting Started With Ethernet/IP and Rheonics SME
Modified on: Wed, 7 Jun, 2023 at 5:26 PM

The purpose of this article is to explain which hardware and configuration are essential for a successful Ethernet/IP implementation.

1. When I order the sensor, have I already purchased Ethernet/IP?
No, you will need to order the Ethernet/IP communication protocol prior to the shipment of the sensor. To order the Ethernet/IP communication protocol remember to check the C7-Ethernet/IP option (https://rheonics.com/request-for-quotation/srv/) in the RFQ page. Ethernet/IP is available on all Rheonics standard viscometers and density meters that use SME electronics. The video below shows the steps to be followed to add Ethernet/IP to the device configuration:

getting started with ethernetip
rheonics
Watch on

2. Can I order Ethernet/IP after a sensor is shipped?
Yes, communication protocols like Ethernet/IP can be ordered later. Contact Rheonics sales to order.

3. Cabling
When Ethernet/IP protocol is enabled on Rheonics Sensors, full duplex communication at 100 Mbps is possible. Ethernet cables that comply with the Ethernet/IP protocol, such as standard ethernet cables or ruggedized ethernet cables, are valid for use in factory automation with physical layers based on 4/2/1-pair Ethernet.
key_facts: The Ethernet/IP communication protocol must be ordered prior to the sensor's shipment by selecting the C7-Ethernet/IP option on the RFQ page.
Ethernet/IP is available on all Rheonics standard viscometers and density meters that use SME electronics.
Ethernet/IP communication protocols can be ordered after a sensor has been shipped by contacting Rheonics sales.
Rheonics Sensors with Ethernet/IP protocol enabled support full duplex communication at 100 Mbps.
Ethernet cables compliant with the Ethernet/IP protocol, including standard or ruggedized types, are valid for use in factory automation when based on 4/2/1-pair Ethernet physical layers.

chunks.page_summary: This page details Ethernet cabling options and their specifications for industrial applications.
chunks.extracted_text:
Figure 1. Ethernet/IP cabling for industrial applications.[1]

Cabling options:
• Standard Ethernet cable options with Cat 5, 5e, Cat 6, 6a, or Cat 7 cables, would meet the requirements based on the achievable speed of 100Mbps in 100 meters, still, specifications must be verified by the integrator.

Figure 2. Flat Ethernet cable[2]

UTP Category | Data Rate | Application | Max.Length
CAT5 | 100Mbps | FastEthernet | 100m
CAT5e | 1Gbps | FastEthernet | 100m
CAT6 | 10Gbps | Gigabit Ethernet | 100m
CAT6a | 10Gbps | Gigabit Ethernet | 100m
CAT7 | 10Gbps | Gigabit Ethernet | 100m

• Industrial Ethernet cable: Ruggedized Ethernet cables, built for the factory floor.
key_facts: Standard Ethernet cable options (Cat 5, 5e, 6, 6a, or 7) can meet requirements for an achievable speed of 100Mbps over 100 meters.
Industrial Ethernet cables are ruggedized and designed for use on the factory floor.
diagram_labels: Figure 1. Ethernet/IP cabling for industrial applications.[1]
warnings_notes: Specifications for standard Ethernet cable options must be verified by the integrator.

chunks.page_summary: This page describes the specifications for Ethernet cables used with Rheonics devices and provides detailed information about obtaining and using EDS (Electronic Data Sheet) files for their Ethernet/IP compatible inline viscometers and density sensors.
product_names: Rheonics inline viscometer and density meter
chunks.extracted_text:
Figure 3. Ruggedized ethernet cable [3]
The selection of the Ethernet cable is up to the integrator, but an Ethernet cable that complies with transmission speeds of 100Mbps is enough.

4. EDS File
Rheonics provides an “EDS file” file that can be used with the different sensor types which are Ethernet/IP compatible. The EDS file is a text file that contains configuration data for specific device types, it describes how a device can be used on an Ethernet/IP network. An EDS file conveys the identity information needed for a network tool to recognize a device. For Ethernet/IP scanners, the EDS file contains information about the Ethernet/IP adapter and the input message produced by the Ethernet/IP adapter and the output message consumed by the adapter. Download our EDS file based on the sensor type that you have selected here: https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000393236-ethernetip-eds-file-for-rheonics-inline-viscometer-and-density-meter

Figure 4. Overview of Rheonics device.
The EDS file selected must always match the sensor type that is under use.
key_facts: Ethernet cables for Rheonics devices should comply with transmission speeds of 100Mbps.
Rheonics provides EDS files for its Ethernet/IP compatible sensor types.
An EDS file is a text file containing configuration data for specific device types.
EDS files describe how a device can be used on an Ethernet/IP network.
EDS files convey identity information for network tools and I/O message details for Ethernet/IP scanners.
Rheonics EDS files for inline viscometers and density meters can be downloaded from the provided support portal link.
The EDS file selected must always match the sensor type that is currently in use.

chunks.page_summary: This page details how to configure Rheonics Ethernet/IP devices for network communication, including specific settings and a list of external references for networking cables, concluding with a general disclaimer.
chunks.extracted_text:
5. Addressing Ethernet/IP networks
Ethernet/IP Devices use a combination of Media Access Control (MAC) addressing and Internet Protocol (IP) addressing for network addressing. Each device must have a unique MAC address and IP address to be identified on the network. The purpose of this combination is to enable Ethernet/IP devices to communicate with each other and the Ethernet/IP controllers.
Rheonics Ethernet/IP devices settings must be set as follows:

Connection: Exclusive owner
Keying: Disabled
RPI: 3200ms (recommended)

References
1. https://www.omnicable.com/products/bel187923a/ (https://www.omnicable.com/products/bel187923a/)
2. https://www.walmart.ca/en/ip/Cat-6-Ethernet-Cable-5ft-Flat-Internet-Network-Cord-Cat6-Ethernet-Patch-Cable-Short-Black-Computer-Cable/PRD519LHF8V3IDH (https://www.walmart.ca/en/ip/Cat-6-Ethernet-Cable-5ft-Flat-Internet-Network-Cord-Cat6-Ethernet-Patch-Cable-Short-Black-Computer-Cable/PRD519LHF8V3IDH)
3. https://www.milestek.com/ip68-ruggedized-cat6-cable-plug-to-jack-e-nickel-finish-24awg-str-cm-cmx-fr-tpe-blk-w-dust-caps-0-5m (https://www.milestek.com/ip68-ruggedized-cat6-cable-plug-to-jack-e-nickel-finish-24awg-str-cm-cmx-fr-tpe-blk-w-dust-caps-0-5m)

key_facts: Ethernet/IP Devices utilize a combination of Media Access Control (MAC) and Internet Protocol (IP) addressing for network identification.
Each Ethernet/IP device must possess a unique MAC and IP address for proper network identification.
The primary goal of combining MAC and IP addressing is to facilitate communication between Ethernet/IP devices and their respective controllers.
Rheonics Ethernet/IP devices have specific network settings that must be applied: Connection as 'Exclusive owner', Keying as 'Disabled', and RPI as '3200ms (recommended)'.
